what: M=64 fused path: BLOCK_SIZE_K=384 with num_stages=2, keeping 16x128 tile and waves_per_eu=2
why: The 64x7168x2048 leaderboard shape currently uses BSK=256 (8 K-iterations). v089 showed BSK=512 is too coarse, and the new BSK=128 benchmark failed structurally in the preshuffle kernel. BSK=384 is the only untested midpoint: 6 K-iterations instead of 8, lower loop/control overhead than 256, but still enough chunking to preserve double-buffered overlap unlike 512.
override: Prior M64 block-size attempts covered BSK=256, BSK=512, BSN/BSM changes, and an invalid BSK=128 path. BSK=384 for the 16x128 fused path has not been tested.

what: M=64 fused path: keep 16x128x256 tile but reduce num_warps from 4 to 2
why: The current M=64 winner uses BSM=16 BSN=128 BSK=256 NW=4 NS=2. Prior NW=2 failures were on different tile families (BSN=64 / autotuned configs), so they only show that small-N tiles are bad. On the current 16x128 path, NW=2 may reduce register pressure while preserving the same 224-block grid and double-buffered K pipeline.
override: Previous NW=2 tests changed tile shape to BSN=64. This tests NW=2 on the existing 16x128x256 M64 path, which has not been isolated before.
